entries.df2855f236056de67edc1c49744ed2f4fd626b17: # CSS: progress-circle `.instui-progress-circle` — A circular progress ring driven by a `--value` (0–100) custom property. The ring is a `conic-gradient` donut painted on `::before` and clipped with a radial-gradient mask; the `--value` custom property drives the arc. **Forrás:** [progress-circle.css](https://github.com/thedannywahl/pantoken/blob/main/formats/components/src/components/progress-circle.css) ## Accessibility Give it role="img" and an aria-label stating the percentage, since the ring is drawn in CSS. ## Usage ```css @import "@pantoken/components/components.css"; @import "@pantoken/components/progress-circle.css"; ``` ## Példák ```html 25% ``` ## Modifiers | Modifier | Leírás | | --- | --- | | `.-color-brand` | Brand meter colour. | | `.-color-danger` | Danger meter colour. | | `.-color-info` | Informational meter colour. | | `.-color-primary-inverse` | On-dark (primary inverse) meter colour. | | `.-color-success` | Success meter colour. | | `.-color-warning` | Warning meter colour. | | `.-meter-color-alert` | Elavult — use `.-color-warning`. | | `.-meter-color-brand` | Elavult — use `.-color-brand`. | | `.-meter-color-danger` | Elavult — use `.-color-danger`. | | `.-meter-color-info` | Elavult — use `.-color-info`. | | `.-meter-color-success` | Elavult — use `.-color-success`. | | `.-meter-color-warning` | Elavult — use `.-color-warning`. | | `.-size-large` | Large. Long-form alias of `-size-lg`. | | `.-size-lg` | Large. | | `.-size-sm` | Small. | | `.-size-small` | Small. Long-form alias of `-size-sm`. | | `.-size-x-small` | Extra small. Long-form alias of `-size-xs`. | | `.-size-xs` | Extra small. | ## Parts | Part | Leírás | | --- | --- | | `.value` | The value text centred in the ring's hole. | ## Pseudo-elements | Pseudo-element | Leírás | | --- | --- | | `::before` | Draws the ring itself: a conic-gradient donut clipped with a radial mask, whose arc tracks the `--value` custom property. | ## Custom properties | Tulajdonság | Type | Default | Leírás | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `--pantoken-pc-fill` | `` | — | The filled arc (meter) colour; the -color-* modifiers set it. | | `--pantoken-pc-stroke` | `` | — | The ring's stroke width; the -size-* modifiers set it. | | `--pantoken-pc-track` | `` | — | The unfilled track colour. | | `--value` | `` | `0` | The progress percentage (0–100) that drives the arc; registered with @property so it can transition. | ## Tokens consumed | Token | Type | Value | | --- | --- | --- | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-color` | `` | `light-dark(#273540, #ffffff)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-color-inverse` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #1C222B)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-font-family` | `[ \| ]#` | `Atkinson Hyperlegible Next, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-font-weight` | `` | `600` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-large-size` | `` | `9em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-large-stroke-width` | `` | `0.875em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-line-height` | `` | `125%` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-medium-size` | `` | `7em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-medium-stroke-width` | `` | `0.625em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-brand` | `` | `light-dark(#1D354F, #EEF4FD)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-brand-inverse` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #6A7883)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-danger` | `` | `#E62429` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-danger-inverse` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #6A7883)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-info` | `` | `#2B7ABC` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-info-inverse` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #6A7883)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-success` | `` | `#03893D` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-success-inverse` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #6A7883)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-warning` | `` | `#CF4A00` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-meter-color-warning-inverse` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #6A7883)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-small-size` | `` | `5em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-small-stroke-width` | `` | `0.5em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-track-color` | `` | `light-dark(#ffffff, #10141A)` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-track-color-inverse` | `` | `#00000000` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-x-small-size` | `` | `3em` | | `--instui-component-progress-circle-x-small-stroke-width` | `` | `0.2em` | ## Browser support - Registers `--value` with `@property` (so the arc can transition) and paints with CSS `mask` and `conic-gradient`; where `@property` is unsupported the ring still renders but won't animate. ## Related - [progress](/api/css/progress.md) — The linear bar form of the same determinate progress.

# swift

`@pantoken/swift` — emit Instructure design tokens as Swift via Style Dictionary.

This is pantoken's native proof: it flattens the IR to concrete, single-mode values
(`@pantoken/core`'s `toStyleDictionary`), keeps the natively-typed tokens (colours, dimensions,
numbers), and hands them to `@pantoken/sd-config`. Swapping `platform` to `flutter`/`compose`
reuses the same path.

## Interfészek

- [GenerateSwiftOptions](interfaces/GenerateSwiftOptions.md)

# Függvény: focusOutlineCss()

> **focusOutlineCss**(`options?`): `string`

Build the focus-outline block: the `--instui-focus-outline-*` token defs plus the ring rules.
Baked into `base.css` (so focusables get the ring out of the box), and reusable by other layered
outputs (e.g. the Pendo renderer) via the `selector`/`tokenSelector` options.

```demo
self:focus-outline
```

## Paraméterek

### options?

`selector` — the focusable selector; `tokenSelector` — where the token defs land
  (default `:where(:root)`).

#### selector?

`string`

#### tokenSelector?

`string`

## Visszatérés

`string`

The CSS string.
entries.e3039bf99302739f4fb223b7673abb7c682d5c46:
# CSS: focus

`:focus-visible` — The focus-outline system: the `--instui-focus-outline-*` custom properties (declared on `:root`) plus the `:focus-visible` ring every focusable gets, and opt-in tuning classes.

**Forrás:** [focus.ts](https://github.com/thedannywahl/pantoken/blob/main/formats/components/src/declarations/focus.ts)

## Accessibility

Every focusable element gets a `:focus-visible` outline ring so keyboard users can see what's focused.

## Usage

```css
@import "@pantoken/components/base.css";
```

## Demo

```demo
self:focus-outline
```

## Példák

```html
<button class="instui-button -focus-color-danger">Delete</button>
```

## Modifiers

| Modifier | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `.-focus-color-danger` | Danger-coloured ring. |
| `.-focus-color-inverse` | Inverse (on-dark) ring. |
| `.-focus-color-success` | Success-coloured ring. |
| `.-focus-position-inset` | Draw the ring inset, inside the element's edge. |
| `.-focus-within` | Ring the element while a descendant is focused. |
| `.-without-focus-animation` | Disable the ring's grow-in animation. |

## Custom properties

| Tulajdonság | Type | Default | Leírás |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--instui-focus-outline-color` | — | — | The ring colour once focus is visible (the themed info colour). |
| `--instui-focus-outline-color-danger` | — | — | The ring colour for the `-focus-color-danger` modifier. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-color-inverse` | — | — | The ring colour for the `-focus-color-inverse` (on-dark) modifier. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-color-start` | — | — | The resting, transparent ring colour the outline transitions from. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-color-success` | — | — | The ring colour for the `-focus-color-success` modifier. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-inset` | — | — | The outline offset used when `-focus-position-inset` draws the ring inside the edge. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-offset` | — | — | The gap between the element's edge and the ring when focused. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-radius` | — | — | The ring's corner radius when focused. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-style` | — | — | The outline line style (solid). |
| `--instui-focus-outline-transition` | — | — | The transition that animates the ring's colour and offset in. |
| `--instui-focus-outline-width` | — | — | The outline width of the ring. |

## Tokens consumed

| Token | Type | Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--instui-border-radius-md` | `<length>` | `0.5rem` |
| `--instui-component-shared-tokens-focus-outline-danger-color` | `<color>` | `#CF1F24` |
| `--instui-component-shared-tokens-focus-outline-info-color` | `<color>` | `light-dark(#2B7ABC, #4798E3)` |
| `--instui-component-shared-tokens-focus-outline-offset` | `<length>` | `0.125rem` |
| `--instui-component-shared-tokens-focus-outline-on-color` | `<color>` | `#ffffff` |
| `--instui-component-shared-tokens-focus-outline-success-color` | `<color>` | `#037D37` |
| `--instui-component-shared-tokens-focus-outline-width` | `<length>` | `0.125rem` |

[pantoken](../../../../../index.md) / [plugins/vite/workspace-orchestrator/src](../index.md) / OrchestratorOptions

# Interfész: OrchestratorOptions

Beállítások a [workspaceOrchestrator](../functions/workspaceOrchestrator.md) számára.

## Tulajdonságok

### upstream

> **upstream**: readonly [`UpstreamNode`](UpstreamNode.md)[]

Az upstream munkaterület függőségi gráfja.

***

### hmrWatchPaths?

> `optional` **hmrWatchPaths?**: readonly `string`[]

A Vite `server.watcher` beállításához hozzáadandó útvonalak, hogy a HMR aktiválódjon, amikor a lefordított kimenet frissül (pl. egy csomag `dist` vagy `generated` könyvtára).

***

### reloadWatchPaths?

> `optional` **reloadWatchPaths?**: readonly `string`[]

Natív `fs.watch` eszközzel figyelendő útvonalak modul-érvénytelenítés kiváltásához. A `hmrWatchPaths` (amely a chokidar-ra épül) helyett ezeket a Node natív `fs.watch` eszközével figyeljük — amely megbízhatóan észleli a pnpm-mel szimlinkelt munkaterületi `dist` könyvtárak változásait. Változás esetén egy szintetikus `"change"` esemény kerül kibocsátásra a `server.watcher` útvonalon, így a modulgráf érvénytelenítve lesz.

***

### debounceMs?

> `optional` **debounceMs?**: `number`

Debounce késleltetés ezredmásodpercben az újraépítés kiváltása előtt (alapértelmezés: 200).

***

### fileServers?

> `optional` **fileServers?**: readonly [`FileServerEntry`](FileServerEntry.md)[]

Opcionális statikus fájlkiszolgáló middleware bejegyzések.

```ts
import { toXcodeImageset } from "@pantoken/core";
```

# next

`@pantoken/next` — a Next.js config adapter.

Instructure UI ships ESM packages that Next must transpile (`transpilePackages`), the #1 papercut
when running InstUI on Next. `withPantoken` merges the InstUI packages into that list. Wrap your
`next.config`, then import `@pantoken/css` in your root layout for the tokens.

## Interfészek

- [NextConfigLike](interfaces/NextConfigLike.md)
- [WithPantokenOptions](interfaces/WithPantokenOptions.md)

## Változók

- [INSTUI\_TRANSPILE\_PACKAGES](variables/INSTUI_TRANSPILE_PACKAGES.md)

## Függvények

- [withPantoken](functions/withPantoken.md)

## Hivatkozások

### default

Renames and re-exports [withPantoken](functions/withPantoken.md)

[pantoken](../../../../index.md) / colors

# colors

`@pantoken/plugin-colors` — CSS-only color math that mirrors the InstUI `@instructure/ui-color-utils`
helpers (`alpha`, `darken`, `lighten`, `overlayColors`).

InstUI computes those at build time with `tinycolor2` against concrete hex values. pantoken emits
`var(--instui-*)` references that resolve in the browser (often through `light-dark()`), so the
math has to happen in CSS instead. Each helper returns a CSS color string built from
[`color-mix()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/CSS/color_value/color-mix) and
[relative color syntax](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_colors/Relative_colors) —
both Baseline modern-browser features — so a single expression tracks the token even when it's a
scheme-dependent `light-dark()` pair. Use them wherever you'd otherwise hard-code a derived shade.

## Példa

```ts
import { alpha, darken } from "@pantoken/plugin-colors";

// A subtle brand-tinted hover, derived from the brand token (mirrors InstUI's ghost hover).
const hover = alpha(darken("var(--instui-color-institutional-brand-button-primary-bgd)", 10), 10);
// → "color-mix(in srgb, hsl(from var(--…brand-…) h s calc(l - 10)) 10%, transparent)"
```

## Típusaliasok

- [CssColor](type-aliases/CssColor.md)

## Függvények

- [alpha](functions/alpha.md)
- [darken](functions/darken.md)
- [lighten](functions/lighten.md)
- [overlayColors](functions/overlayColors.md)
- [onColor](functions/onColor.md)

entries.ef1ade5a6fac7d3c47383f0a93156a95c44eac3c:
[pantoken](../../../../index.md) / workspace-orchestrator

# workspace-orchestrator

`@pantoken/vite-workspace-orchestrator` — a Vite dev-server plugin that watches upstream workspace
packages and rebuilds them (and their dependents) when source changes.

During development, changes to a local workspace package normally need a manual rebuild before the
dev server reflects them. This plugin automates that: it watches each upstream package's source
with native `fs.watch` (not Vite's chokidar, which filters paths outside the project root),
debounces rapid changes, rebuilds in topological order, and registers built output with Vite's
watcher so the browser reloads. In pantoken's docs it keeps the generated CSS (`@pantoken/css`,
`@pantoken/components`) fresh as you edit the libraries, instead of only at build time.

It applies during `serve` only, so production builds are untouched.

## Példa

```ts
import { resolve } from "node:path";
import { workspaceOrchestrator } from "@pantoken/vite-workspace-orchestrator";

workspaceOrchestrator({
  upstream: [
    {
      name: "@pantoken/components",
      dir: resolve(root, "formats/components"),
      watchPaths: [resolve(root, "formats/components/src")],
      build: ["pnpm", "run", "build"],
      dependents: [],
    },
  ],
  hmrWatchPaths: [resolve(root, "formats/components/generated")],
});
```

## Interfészek

- [UpstreamNode](interfaces/UpstreamNode.md)
- [FileServerEntry](interfaces/FileServerEntry.md)
- [OrchestratorOptions](interfaces/OrchestratorOptions.md)

## Függvények

- [matchesFilters](functions/matchesFilters.md)
- [workspaceOrchestrator](functions/workspaceOrchestrator.md)

[pantoken](../../../index.md) / foundation

# foundation

`@pantoken/foundation` — theme Foundation for Sites with Instructure tokens.

Foundation is Sass-first, so this package ships two layers. [toFoundationSettings](functions/toFoundationSettings.md) emits a
`_settings`-style Sass partial that points Foundation's setting variables at `var(--instui-*)`, so
a Sass build compiles the Instructure look while keeping runtime theming through the same custom
properties. [toFoundationCss](functions/toFoundationCss.md) emits a thin CSS overlay that themes the common compiled
classes (`.button`, `.callout`, links) the same way — useful when you consume stock Foundation CSS
and just want to layer Instructure colors on top without recompiling.

## Példa

```ts
import { foundationSettings, foundationCss } from "@pantoken/foundation";
// foundationSettings → a Sass partial; foundationCss → a runtime overlay.
```

## Interfészek

- [ToFoundationSettingsOptions](interfaces/ToFoundationSettingsOptions.md)
- [ToFoundationCssOptions](interfaces/ToFoundationCssOptions.md)

## Változók

- [FOUNDATION\_TO\_INSTUI](variables/FOUNDATION_TO_INSTUI.md)
- [foundationSettings](variables/foundationSettings.md)
- [foundationCss](variables/foundationCss.md)

## Függvények

- [toFoundationSettings](functions/toFoundationSettings.md)
- [toFoundationCss](functions/toFoundationCss.md)

## Hivatkozások

### default

entries.f284a26829be3648825c8ed4ce04585fb2330ff1:
[pantoken](../../../../index.md) / [packages/utils/src](../index.md) / tokenUtilitiesCss

# Függvény: tokenUtilitiesCss()

> **tokenUtilitiesCss**(`groups`, `options?`): `string`

Build token-driven utility classes: one class per token, applying it to its natural CSS property. The
token's `--instui-` tail is the class name, so `--instui-font-weight-body-strong` under property
`font-weight` yields
`.<prefix>-font-weight-body-strong { font-weight: var(--instui-font-weight-body-strong); }`. Use it for
every "one token → one property" family (font-family/weight, line-height, border-radius, border-width,
opacity, box-shadow). Colour and spacing keep their own builders — one token maps to several
properties there. Pass the token names per property (e.g. filtered from `@pantoken/tokens`).

```demo
self:token-utilities
```

## Paraméterek

### groups

readonly [`TokenUtilityGroup`](../interfaces/TokenUtilityGroup.md)[]

one [TokenUtilityGroup](../interfaces/TokenUtilityGroup.md) per CSS property.

### options?

[`UtilityOptions`](../interfaces/UtilityOptions.md) = `{}`

[UtilityOptions](../interfaces/UtilityOptions.md).

## Visszatérés

`string`

The CSS string.

# Függvény: mergePlugin()

> **mergePlugin**(...`plugins`): [`PantokenPlugin`](../../../core/src/interfaces/PantokenPlugin.md)

Több plugint egyesít eggyé, balról jobbra összevonva őket az [extendPlugin](extendPlugin.md) segítségével.

## Példa

**Társ pluginek egyesítése eggyé**

```ts
import { capabilitiesOf, definePlugin, mergePlugin } from "@pantoken/plugin-kit";

const brand = definePlugin({ name: "brand", tokens: (c) => c.tokens });
const glyphs = definePlugin({ name: "glyphs", icons: (c) => c.add({ name: "star" }) });

const combined = mergePlugin(brand, glyphs);
capabilitiesOf(combined); // → ["tokens", "icons"]
```

entries.f55176967eb885250eab69a0b8d38b574897a3a3:
[pantoken](../../../../../index.md) / [plugins/pantoken/colors/src](../index.md) / overlayColors

# Függvény: overlayColors()

> **overlayColors**(`base`, `overlay`, `percent?`): `string`

Flatten `overlay` (at `percent`% strength) over an opaque `base` — a CSS-only stand-in for
ui-color-utils `overlayColors`. That helper source-over-composites two RGBA colors into one opaque
result; the common case (a translucent tint over a solid surface) is exactly a two-color
`color-mix()`. General RGBA-over-RGBA compositing can't be a single CSS color, so this covers the
opaque-base case only.

## Paraméterek

### base

`string`

The opaque background color.

### overlay

`string`

The color laid over it.

### percent?

`number` = `50`

How much of `overlay` shows through, 0–100 (default `50`).

## Visszatérés

`string`

A `color-mix()` expression.

## Példa

```ts
overlayColors("var(--surface)", "var(--brand)", 12);
```

# Változó: dateTimeInput

`<instui-date-time-input>` — a nested `<instui-date-input>` plus a native time field. A change to

# vue

`@pantoken/vue` — a Vue plugin over `@pantoken/web-components`.

`.instui-simple-select` — A styled native `<select>` with a caret, matching the text-input states and sizes.

**Forrás:** [simple-select.ts](https://github.com/thedannywahl/pantoken/blob/main/formats/components/src/components/simple-select.ts)

```css
@import "@pantoken/components/simple-select.css";
```

```html
  <option>Apple</option>

  <option>Pear</option>
```

# CSS: button

`.instui-button` — An accessible action control, styled from the token palette; primary by default.

The AI variants layer two gradients — a padding-box fill and a border-box stroke — for their frame, and `-color-ai-secondary` can't paint gradient text and a fill at once, so its centre stays transparent at rest and fills on hover or active. Ghost hover and active derive a low-opacity, slightly-darkened brand wash rather than using the raw hover-background tokens, which would print same-colour-on-same-colour text.

**Forrás:** [button.ts](https://github.com/thedannywahl/pantoken/blob/main/formats/components/src/components/button.ts)

## Accessibility

Drive the `-toggle` variant's pressed state with `aria-pressed`, and mark a disabled button with `aria-disabled` (or the native `disabled`).

## Usage

```css
@import "@pantoken/components/components.css";
@import "@pantoken/components/button.css";
```

## Demo

```demo
self:button
```

## Példák

```html
<button class="instui-button">Primary</button>
```

## Modifiers

| Modifier | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `.-color-ai` | An AI action. |
| `.-color-ai-secondary` | A lower-emphasis AI action. |
| `.-color-danger` | A destructive action. |
| `.-color-primary-inverse` | Primary action for dark backgrounds. |
| `.-color-secondary` | A lower-emphasis secondary action. |
| `.-color-success` | A positive/confirming action. |
| `.-color-tertiary` | A text-style action (no fill or border until hover). |
| `.-condensed` | Tighter padding for dense toolbars. |
| `.-display-block` | Full-width block button. |
| `.-ghost` | Outline (ghost) style: a border in the colour's ghost tokens, no fill. |
| `.-icon-*` | Render a glyph from the icon set before the label (e.g. `-icon-arrow-right`), painted in the button's text colour; pair with `-shape-square`/`-shape-circle` for an icon-only button. |
| `.-shape-circle` | A round icon button. |
| `.-shape-square` | A square icon button. |
| `.-size-large` | Large. Long-form alias of `-size-lg`. |
| `.-size-lg` | Large. |
| `.-size-sm` | Small. |
| `.-size-small` | Small. Long-form alias of `-size-sm`. |
| `.-toggle` | A pressed-state toggle button (drive with aria-pressed). |
| `.-without-background` | Drop the fill (ghost). |
| `.-without-border` | Remove the border. |

## Pseudo-elements

| Pseudo-element | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `::after` | The AI-secondary gradient border ring, masked to just the button's frame. |
| `::before` | The AI glyph, added automatically to AI buttons and masked in the variant's own colour. |

## States

| State | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `:disabled` | — |
| `:state(pressed)` | — |

## Tokens consumed

| Token | Type | Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--instui-border-width-md` | `<length>` | `0.125rem` |
| `--instui-border-width-sm` | `<length>` | `0.0625rem` |
| `--instui-color-background-interactive-action-ai-bottom-gradient-active` | `<color>` | `#01626E` |
| `--instui-color-background-interactive-action-ai-bottom-gradient-base` | `<color>` | `#027887` |
| `--instui-color-background-interactive-action-ai-bottom-gradient-hover` | `<color>` | `#00828E` |

[pantoken](../../../index.md) / wordpress

# wordpress

`@pantoken/wordpress` — Instructure design tokens as a WordPress block-theme `theme.json`.

[toThemeJson](functions/toThemeJson.md) converts any IR; [themeJson](variables/themeJson.md) is the ready-made `rebrand` document. The
CLI can write it into a theme directory: `pantoken generate wordpress --out ./my-theme`.

## Interfészek

- [ThemeJson](interfaces/ThemeJson.md)
- [ToThemeJsonOptions](interfaces/ToThemeJsonOptions.md)

## Típusaliasok

- [Mode](type-aliases/Mode.md)

## Változók

- [themeJson](variables/themeJson.md)

## Függvények

- [toThemeJson](functions/toThemeJson.md)

## Hivatkozások

### default

Renames and re-exports [themeJson](variables/themeJson.md)

entries.ff657589a1941faf977c33b39937c914c080239b:
# CSS: toggle-details

`.instui-toggle-details` — A styled native `<details>` disclosure with a rotating chevron.

Built on the native `<details>` element, so the browser drives open and close plus keyboard support; the CSS only hides the default marker and supplies the rotating chevron.

**Forrás:** [toggle-details.css](https://github.com/thedannywahl/pantoken/blob/main/formats/components/src/components/toggle-details.css)

## Usage

```css
@import "@pantoken/components/components.css";
@import "@pantoken/components/toggle-details.css";
```

## Példák

```html
<details class="instui-toggle-details" open>
  <summary>What ships in this package?</summary>
  Class-based component styles, built from the Instructure tokens, plus a prose layer.
</details>
```

## Structure

```text
.instui-toggle-details
  summary
```

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  n0[".instui-toggle-details"]:::cssdoc-root
  n1("summary"):::cssdoc-part
  n0 --> n1
  classDef cssdoc-root fill:#eef2ff,stroke:#6366f1,color:#1e1b4b;
  classDef cssdoc-part fill:#f8fafc,stroke:#94a3b8,color:#0f172a;
  classDef cssdoc-slot fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#4ade80,color:#14532d;
  classDef cssdoc-component fill:#fff7ed,stroke:#fb923c,color:#7c2d12;
```

## Modifiers

| Modifier | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `.-chevron-end` | Place the chevron after the summary. |
| `.-size-large` | Large. Long-form alias of `-size-lg`. |
| `.-size-lg` | Large. |
| `.-size-sm` | Small. |
| `.-size-small` | Small. Long-form alias of `-size-sm`. |
| `.-variant-filled` | Filled (surface) variant. |

## Pseudo-elements

| Pseudo-element | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `::before` | Draws the summary's disclosure chevron, a masked glyph that rotates to point down when the disclosure is open. |

## States

| State | Leírás |
| --- | --- |
| `:state(open)` | — |

| `--instui-component-toggle-details-font-size-large` | `<length>` | `1.25rem` |

entries.ffceff6baed67907ea0f0886780c12ff9ccc7fb1:
[pantoken](../../../../index.md) / [packages/utils/src](../index.md) / tokenNames

# Függvény: tokenNames()

> **tokenNames**(`ir`): `Set`\<`string`\>

Az IR által definiált tokennevek halmaza.

## Paraméterek

### ir

readonly [`Token`](../../../core/src/interfaces/Token.md)[]

## Visszatérési érték

`Set`\<`string`\>

## Példa

```ts
import { tokenNames } from "@pantoken/utils";
import type { Token } from "@pantoken/model";

const ir: Token[] = [
  { name: "--instui-leaf", syntax: "<color>", inherits: true, value: "#0374B5" },
];
tokenNames(ir); // → Set { "--instui-leaf" }
```
